title: In Mali, the jihadists' goal 'is not taking Bamako, but changing the people in power'
subhead: The offensive by the Al-Qaeda-affiliated group JNIM threatens Mali's capital, which is under blockade. In an interview with Le Monde, analyst Jean-Hervé Jezequel examines the jihadists' objectives.

title: Trump says US will hike tariffs on EU cars and trucks to 25%
Baguettes take center stage on France's Labor Day as government encourages bakers to work
In Brazil's 'slaughterhouse capital,' workers are under pressure: 'The companies want us to be robots'
subhead: To cope with the demand for meat, expected to increase under the EU-Mercosur agreement, slaughterhouses recruit from among Indigenous populations and immigrants. Employees report harsh working conditions, including a grueling pace, cold temperatures and faulty machines.

title: Ruth Gruenthal, 103-year-old Holocaust survivor, reclaims her German citizenship
subhead: Eighty-six years after being stripped of her citizenship, the American retiree living in New York decided to have it 'restored' out of fear of Donald Trump's authoritarian tendencies.

title: Seafood and veggie tide inundates dining tables on the Basque coast
subhead: Young talents focused on vegetables and fish have elevated the gastronomic offerings in Biarritz and its neighboring town Bidart, in a region traditionally passionate about meat.

title: 'I continue to protest, but with a knot in my stomach': How Sweden is discouraging foreigners from taking climate action
subhead: After standing trial for participating in a protest against private jets, French national Isabelle Letellier was also denied citizenship. This double penalty highlights how Stockholm has toughened its stance toward environmental activists.

'It is difficult to see what the French government can achieve in the coming year'
In a small French village, a father is charged with sexual abuse of 34 victims aged 3 to 9
subhead: A tree, a rope, a letter. After surviving a suicide attempt, a man revealed he had committed numerous crimes against children. After a year-long and controversial investigation, he was charged, on April 10, with rape and sexual assault of minors and recording child pornography.
byline: Jérémie Pham-Lê and  Lorraine de Foucher

title: 'In the war between the US and Iran, both sides are convinced they control the clock'
subhead: Tehran believes Trump needs a deal; Washington thinks the Islamic Republic cannot survive without one, explains Alain Frachon in his column for Le Monde.

title: 'Writing helps us organize our ideas. How will we understand the world if we let AI do the writing?'
subhead: Le Monde columnist Philippe Bernard explores the questions raised by the decline of writing and the rise of artificial intelligence for our cognitive abilities.

title: 'Céline Dion will generate the equivalent of one-fifth of the economic impact of the Paris Olympics'
